citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
There is one expired bottle of Albon suspension (expiration date of 12/2024) and one expired box of vaccines (expiration
date 09/22/2025) in one of the dog housing buildings. The licensee disposed of both expired drugs when found during
inspection.
The use of expired medications or medications with an unknown expiration date is not considered to be a safe or
appropriate method to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases and injuries because expired drugs may be
ineffective or produce other undesired effects. Animals may experience discomfort or distress due to unrelieved
symptoms or unexpected side effects from the use of expired medication, and vaccines may fail to protect from disease.
The program for providing adequate veterinary care shall include the use of appropriate methods to prevent, control,
diagnose, and treat diseases and injuries. This includes establishing and following procedures to ensure that expired
medications are removed and cannot be administered to animals.
CORRECTED DURING THE TIME OF THIS INSPECTION ON 04/23/2026

There are three adult dogs at the facility without a method of identification on their bodies: one adult female poodle named
"Fifi", one male bichon-poodle cross named "Bam Bam" and one adult female bichon-poodle cross named "Pebbles".
All dogs and cats must have identification in an acceptable form and placement as described in Part 2, Subpart E in order
to facilitate tracing of all animals and their humane use. The licensee must ensure that all live dogs and cats held on the
premises, purchased or otherwise acquired, sold or otherwise disposed of, or removed from the premises for delivery to a
research facility, exhibitor or another dealer, or for sale, through an auction sale or to any person for use as a pet, shall be
identified by an official tag of the type described in Part 2, Subpart E, Section 2.51 affixed to the animal's collar around the
neck; microchips and distinctive, legible tattoo markings (if acknowledged and approved by the Administrator) are also
acceptable means of identification.
TO BE CORRECTED BY 04/29/2026

***Although there are some records to document the acquisition information for all dogs at the facility, the licensee does
not have an APHIS Form 7005 for five adult dogs: one male bichon-poodle cross named "Bam Bam" (no ID number or
microchip), one female bichon-poodle cross named "Pebbles" (no ID number or microchip), one female bichon named
"Bubbles" (microchip #990000012123220), one female bichon named "Bambie" (microchip #990000013886254) and one
female poodle named "Fifi" (no ID number or microchip).
***The licensee does not have disposition records or an APHIS Form 7006 for one adult female maltese named "Lola"
(microchip #900215006052015) that is no longer at the facility; she is now a pet and belongs to a family member.
Complete and accurate records are needed to ensure each animal's identity can be known with certainty and to facilitate
the ability to trace all animals; the licensee shall use Record of Acquisition and Dogs and Cats on Hand (APHIS Form
7005) and Record of Disposition of Dogs and Cats (APHIS Form 7006) to make, keep and maintain the information
required under Part 2, Subpart G, Section 2.75(a)(1). These forms must fully and correctly disclose the required
information concerning each dog or cat purchased or otherwise acquired, owned, held, or otherwise in his or her
possession or under his or her control, or which is transported, euthanized, sold, or otherwise disposed of by the dealer.
TO BE CORRECTED BY 04/29/2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
n

Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
